# CI Dashboard API Instructions for LLMs

This dashboard provides a REST API to monitor, fetch logs, and wait for CI/CD actions to complete on GitHub and Forgejo/Gitea.

## 1. Read All Statuses
Fetch the latest status of all tracked repositories:
`curl -s https://dash.hackedyour.info/api/status`

Returns a JSON array of objects:
[
  {
    "provider": "github|forgejo",
    "owner": "string",
    "repo": "string",
    "status": "success|failure|running|cancelled|error",
    "url": "string (direct link to action)",
    "repo_url": "string",
    "updated_at": "ISO-8601 timestamp",
    "commit_message": "string",
    "custom_links": [{"name": "string", "url": "string"}]
  }
]

## 2. Find Isolated/Targeted Information
Combine `/api/status` with `jq` to target a specific repository:
`curl -s https://dash.hackedyour.info/api/status | jq '.[] | select(.owner=="adamoutler" and .repo=="gemini-webui")'`

To just extract the status:
`curl -s https://dash.hackedyour.info/api/status | jq -r '.[] | select(.owner=="adamoutler" and .repo=="gemini-webui") | .status'`

## 3. Get Logs for a Run
Fetch the raw log output or a link to the log for the latest run:
`curl -s "https://dash.hackedyour.info/api/logs?provider=github&owner=adamoutler&repo=gemini-webui"`

Returns:
{
  "log": "Raw string content of the log or a message linking to the Web UI"
}

## 4. Post/Upload Logs for a Run
You can upload or push logs directly to the dashboard to persist them and make them accessible via the GET `/api/logs` endpoint.
This is especially useful for Forgejo instances that do not expose logs natively via their API.

Upload logs using a POST request:
```bash
curl -X POST "https://dash.hackedyour.info/api/logs?provider=forgejo&owner=adamoutler&repo=plane-mcp-rework" \
     -H "Content-Type: text/plain" \
     --data-binary @job-logs.txt
```
To prevent DDOS and memory exhaustion, the dashboard uses a cyclic buffer to keep only the final 2MB of any uploaded log. Anything larger is automatically truncated, keeping the tail.

## 5. Get Artifacts (Recommended for Forgejo/Gitea)
Since Gitea/Forgejo APIs do not always natively support programmatic log downloading, it is highly recommended that LLMs and AI Agents update the repository's CI workflow file (e.g., `.forgejo/workflows/` or `.github/workflows/`) to **explicitly upload an artifact** (like a build output or test summary) via `actions/upload-artifact@v4`.

You can then retrieve the artifact metadata using:
`curl -s "https://dash.hackedyour.info/api/artifacts?provider=forgejo&owner=adamoutler&repo=plane-mcp-rework"`

This will return a JSON object detailing the artifacts available for download for the latest run, which you can use in place of raw logs if they are inaccessible.

## 6. Wait for a Build (Long-Polling)
Hold the connection open and wait until an in-progress build completes:
`curl -N -s "https://dash.hackedyour.info/api/wait?provider=github&owner=adamoutler&repo=gemini-webui"`

This streams "." characters while the build is "running", "in_progress", "queued", or "waiting", and outputs a final JSON block once the status is terminal (e.g. "success", "failure").
